titleStmt.title: A Transcription of John in 333
GA 333
The New Testament
Das Neue Testament
The Gospel according to John
john

respStmt.name: the International Greek New Testament Project

titleStmt.funder: The Arts and Humanities Research Council

sponsor.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing

publisher.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing
University of Birmingham

availability.p: Licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (CC BY 4.0): available for re-use provided attribution is made to the original creators (the IGNTP and any named participants) and an indication is given of any changes made. See further .

projectDesc.p: This transcription was made by the International Greek New Testament Project in its work towards the Editio Critica Maior of John between 2010 and 2013.

editorialDecl.p: The initial transcription file was a plain text file for use with COLLATE, later converted to XML. The original transcription guidelines can be found at
The plain text file was converted to XML by Catherine Smith using the project's Python conversion scripts 1.0.
The XML specifications used are version 1.4 of the IGNTP Guidelines available at

revisionDesc.change: Transcription changed at 18.28 by David Parker.
Nicetas atttribution of commentary removed from title by David Parker.
20.30 and 20.31 tags (omitted verses at end of witness) deleted by Rachel Kevern.
Unclear and supplied tags altered at 2.5,6,7 and 8 by Rachel Kevern.
το deleted from 5.27 by Rachel Kevern.
The following folios have been reordered to correct the order of the biblical text: 367, 368, 369, 370, 371 and 372 by Rachel Kevern.

αγουσι changed to αγουσιν at 18.28; ος λεγεται deleted at 19.17; α[υτο]υ changed to απ [αυτ]ου at 19.36; whole of 20.17 added; second half of 20.20 added; first half of 20.21 added; θωμα deleted at 20.29 by Rachel Kevern.

ζησεται changed to ζησει at 6.58, τουτων removed at 7.31; ο ιησους deleted from 8.20; second τω changed to του at 8.38; εμαυτω changed to επ αυτω at 11.38; ο changed to ο[λος] at 12.19; by Rachel Kevern.

[αυτον] added at 1.19; 1.31 moved and completed; [ο] ις- added at 1.43; του removed at 1.45; ιουδαιων changed to ιουδαιου at 3.25; παλιν deleted at 4.3; [σου] changed to σου at 4.16; ημελλε changed to εμελλε at 4.47; [απ]εκρινετο changed to [απ]εκρινατο at 5.7; το added at beginning of 5.27; second εγω deleted, [ποιειν] added, ουδεν moved and at οτι ου ζητω το θελημα το εμον αλλα το θελημα του πεμψαντος με πατρος added at 5.30; by Rachel Kevern.
App and ill tags removed at 20.26, 20.27 (x2), 20.28, 20.29, 20.24, 20.25 (x3), 20.21, 20.23 by Rachel Kevern.

ακουσι changed to ακουουσι at 10.27, επεσεν changed to επεσε at 11.32, πυρ το βαλλουσι changed to το πυρ βαλλουσι at 15.6, μεινη deleted from 15.11, ουν changed to ον and editorial note added at 18.9, supplied [κειμενα] deleted at 20.5, ης changed to εις at 20.24, ερχεται removed from * and C at 20.26 by Rachel Kevern.
Word break at 1.15 and 20.25 corrected, καλει removed at 10.3, μαριαν changed to μαρθα at 11.28, αλλα changed to αλλ at 11.54 by Rachel Kevern.
Transcription of κλαυσετε at 16.20 changed, correction at 17.22 deleted by David Parker.
Word break at 17.22 corrected by David Parker.
Correction in 13.5 removed and ed note added by David Parker.
NomSac tag added at 1.47 by David Parker.
Iota adscript removed by David Parker.
Converted from plain text by Catherine Smith using Python conversion scripts 1.0.

body.note: Status:G-A Number: 333Name of book transcribed: JohnFirst folio transcribed: 168rBase text: TRns Transcribed by: Michael A. ClarkTranscription begun: 11 May 2011Transcription finished: 21 Nov 2012 Does this manuscript contain (delete and complete as appropriate): Lectionary annotations - Yes No Initials and other enlarged letters - Yes No Diples (> or >> in the margin next to certain text, indicating that text is a quotation from the Old Testament) - Yes No Are there any special symbols in the margin next to any of the following verses: 5.4 No 7.53 - 8.11 No These verses are missing Other remarks: The original ms ends at 372v and Jn 20.23, with the rest being a supplement.

ab.note: Preceded by του αυτου κυρου νικητας του του σερρων εις
This may not be a lemma
I reconstructed the illegible letters on this and the next line based on 430, which is also Nicetas. Based on what is legible, this makes more sense than the majority reading.
Refer to status note for this verse
This section is mostly illegiblle, so I'm really guessing here.
7.2-10 can be found after 7.37, on folio 249.
There may be commentary here, or maybe not. It could just be smudges.
The end of this and the line above may have been overwritten.
This line is marked with two red lines meeting in a right angle.
These three words have been overwritten.
These three words have been overwritten.
These two words have been overwritten.
The end of this and the line above may have been overwritten.
The end of this line has been overwritten.
The first letters of this and the following five lines have been overwritten.
Part of this word has been overwritten.
These four words have been overwritten.
The beginning of this and the line below have been overwritten.
The beginning of this word has been overwritten.
The end of this line has been overwritten.
The end of this line and the beginning of the next have been overwritten.
Some text in this area has been overwritten.
Parts of this line have been overwritten.
These three words have been overwritten.
Part of this word has been overwritten.
The end of this and part of the line below have been overwritten.
Parts of this and the line below have been overwritten.
Parts of this line above have been overwritten.
Parts of this line above have been overwritten.
Parts of this and the following lines above have been overwritten.
Parts of this line and the lines below have been overwritten.
Parts of this line have been overwritten.
Parts of this line and the lines below have been overwritten.
Parts of this line have been overwritten.
Part of this line has been overwritten.
Part of this line has been overwritten.
Parts of the lower area of this page have been overwritten.
Parts of this page have been overwritten.
Parts of this area have been overwritten.
Parts of this page have been overwritten.
The scribe seems to have made a correction to τ in scribendo.
Parts of this page have been overwritten.
Parts of this page have been overwritten.
Parts of this page have been overwritten.
We think the mark between the ο and ν is an inkblot rather than a letter.
Parts of this page have been overwritten.

div.note: The pages containing chapter 20 have been bound out of order order.

ab.note: Parts of this page have been overwritten.
Parts of this page have been overwritten.
Parts of this page have been overwritten.
Parts of this page have been overwritten.
Parts of this page have been overwritten.
Parts of this page have been overwritten.
